title: Synchronous Blocking I/O in Hot Edit-Time Hook Paths
description: The PreToolUse hook (knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs) is invoked synchronously on every file edit tool (Edit, Write, MultiEdit) to inject narrow knowledge hints. It blocks the main host event loop by performing synchronous filesystem checks and writes (readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, renameSync, unlinkSync via the atomicWrite wrapper in state-store.cjs) on every invocation. In rapid edits or concurrent workspaces this blocks Node's single thread on disk I/O, creating a typing/editing delay for the developer.
fix_direction: Refactor the file persistence helper (state-store.cjs) and hook script to use async operations (fs.promises) or a decoupled background process so writes do not block the active editor loop.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Lock-Reclaim Race Condition and Lock Theft in withFileLock
description: withFileLock manages cross-process advisory locking via O_CREAT|O_EXCL. If a lock becomes stale (exceeding staleMs, e.g. 10s), another process reclaims it by unlink(lockPath) then open(lockPath,'wx'). When the original slow holder finally completes, its unconditional finally block calls unlink(lockPath), deleting the NEW holder's active lock file. This leaves the new holder unprotected, allowing concurrent entry into the critical section and shared-state corruption.
fix_direction: Write a unique process/session token (UUID) into the exclusive lock file on creation; in finally, only unlink if file contents match the current holder's token.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Massive God-File and Overlong Functions in doctor.ts
description: doctor.ts is an 8696-line god-file packing report generation (runDoctorReport), three repair/mutation runners, and dozens of complex low-level inspectors (e.g. inspectL2ManagedBlockDrift spanning lines 3250-4012, over 750 lines). Highly regression-prone, slow to analyze, hard to test in isolation.
fix_direction: Decompose into a doctor/ directory; extract each validator/inspector into its own module (managed-block-drift.ts, orphan-demote.ts, ledger-invariant.ts) behind a consolidated orchestrator.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Global cooldown throttle silences hook nudges across concurrent sessions
description: The hook uses a single GLOBAL cooldown cache file (.fabric/.cache/archive-hint-shown.json) and a single global maintenance last-emit file instead of isolating cooldowns by session_id. With concurrent sessions / multiple editor windows, a nudge in one session updates the global shown cache, immediately resetting the cooldown throttle globally and silently silencing nudges in other active sessions — missed user/AI guidance.
fix_direction: Scope cache filenames/keys by session_id (archive-hint-shown-{sessionId}.json, maintenance-hint-last-emit-{sessionId}), mirroring session-hints-{session_id}.json.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Interactive elements lack custom focus styles (nav/toggle/copy)
description: Navigation links (Readiness, Rules Explain, Timeline, Health), theme toggle, and terminal copy buttons define no focus-visible/custom focus indicator. Under keyboard nav users cannot track focus — WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
fix_direction: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-accent focus-visible:outline-none to all sidebar nav links and active buttons.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Search input removes focus outline without visual fallback (line 139)
description: The search input uses outline-none, suppressing default focus rings, with no custom indicator on focus — fails WCAG 2.4.7 for keyboard-only operators.
fix_direction: Replace outline-none with focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-accent focus:border-transparent focus:outline-none.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Personal-layer (KP-*) metadata leaks into git-committed agents.meta.json
description: buildKnowledgeMeta scans both team-root (.fabric/knowledge/) and personal-root (~/.fabric/knowledge/), merges KT-* and KP-* nodes into one dictionary, and writes .fabric/agents.meta.json in the project root. Since that file is NOT gitignored, developers' personal-layer metadata (absolute home paths, titles, summaries, section headers) is permanently committed to the shared repo. Per-developer personal dirs also cause constant computeRevision mismatches, triggering drift/heal spam.
fix_direction: Exclude KP-* / ~/.fabric/knowledge entries from the shared .fabric/agents.meta.json; persist personal metadata machine-locally (.fabric/.cache/ or ~/.fabric/). Exclude personal entries from computeRevision.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unconditional recursive workspace walk on every read/plan-context request
description: buildKnowledgeMeta is called unconditionally on all read/plan paths (loadActiveMeta / loadActiveMetaOrStale during planContext, recall, getKnowledgeSections). It triggers findFabricVerifyAnnotations -> findTestFiles, which recursively walks the ENTIRE workspace directory + stats all files to inspect annotations. At 10x-100x codebase size, a full-workspace traversal on every read request degrades performance catastrophically.
fix_direction: Cache the workspace test-file list or only re-walk on a file-mutation event from the watcher, instead of a full recursive walk per incoming API request.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Frontmatter YAML Injection in Modify/Approve/Reject Paths
description: During metadata update paths in review.ts, string arrays like tags and relevance_paths are joined with a bare ', ' separator without per-element double-quoting or escaping. If any tag or relevance path contains YAML special characters (']' or newlines), it can escape the array context and inject arbitrary YAML keys/values into the frontmatter block.
fix_direction: Use quoteRelevancePath / quoteIfNeeded on each element of tags and relevance_paths before joining.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Frontmatter Escape via Backslash in quoteIfNeeded (review.ts)
description: quoteIfNeeded in review.ts only replaces double-quotes with escaped double-quotes and does NOT escape backslashes first. A value ending in a backslash ('foo\') yields a trailing backslash escaping the closing quote, letting subsequent content forge arbitrary frontmatter keys. This is the same bug class as the known extract-knowledge.ts:718 issue, present in a separate un-fixed copy.
fix_direction: Escape backslashes first (replace(/\\/g,'\\\\')) before escaping double-quotes, matching quoteRelevancePath in extract-knowledge.ts.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Path Traversal in reverse-unarchive primitive
description: unarchiveKnowledge resolves the caller-supplied archivePathRel via join(projectRoot, archivePathPosix) with no directory confinement / sandboxing. Traversal paths ('../') can access or move files outside the .fabric/.archive/ boundary.
fix_direction: Add path confinement (like resolveSandboxedPath in review.ts) to guarantee the resolved path stays strictly within .fabric/.archive/.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Synchronous Repository Walks and File Reads in Doctor Checks
description: fabric doctor executes deep repository walks to lint the KB and system state. During loop processing it performs synchronous readdirSync, readFileSync, statSync to check all MD entries and session cache files. For large repos or slow storage this synchronous blocking I/O freezes the single-threaded Node loop, causing slow CLI responses and high CPU.
fix_direction: Refactor deep directory inspections and reads in doctor.ts to async readdir/stat/readFile and parallelize with Promise.all().

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Counter overwrite: writeKnowledgeMeta rebuilds without the allocator lock
description: KnowledgeIdAllocator.allocate increments stable_id counters in agents.meta.json inside a withFileLock critical section. But writeKnowledgeMeta (sync/auto-heal/doctor) reads + recomputes via buildKnowledgeMeta and writes back with atomicWriteText WITHOUT acquiring the lock. If it reads before allocate's incremented write lands, it overwrites the meta with stale counters, causing duplicate stable_id allocation and index collisions.
fix_direction: Acquire withFileLock on ${metaPath}.lock in writeKnowledgeMeta before reading/computing/writing the metadata, serializing rebuilds against allocator increments.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Cross-process event loss in ledger rotation and drops
description: rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded and dropEventsFromLedger read events.jsonl, partition/filter, and overwrite via atomicWriteText, serialized only by an in-process queue (ledgerQueue.runExclusive). Across multiple client sessions or CLI/server processes the separate queues do not lock against each other, so one process's rewrite clobbers concurrent appends/rotations.
fix_direction: Wrap the critical sections of rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded and dropEventsFromLedger in withFileLock on events.jsonl.lock for cross-process mutual exclusion.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Torn writes / page-cache corruption from omission of fsync
description: atomicWriteJson persists critical derived files like agents.meta.json but calls atomicWriteText without a truthy fsync option — write temp then rename, no flush. A power loss/crash right after rename can leave unflushed page-cache, corrupting metadata or producing a zero-byte agents.meta.json.
fix_direction: Enable fsync by default for critical metadata/config writes (agents.meta.json, settings.json) in KnowledgeIdAllocator and doctor fixes, forcing fd.datasync before rename.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Boilerplate Copy-Paste Installation Functions for Skills
description: Identical boilerplate install functions exist for all 7 active Skills (installFabricArchiveSkill, installFabricReviewSkill, installFabricImportSkill, installFabricSyncSkill, installFabricStoreSkill, installFabricAuditSkill, installFabricConnectSkill). Each manually reads a template, validates size, maps destinations, runs copyTextIdempotent, and logs. Violates DRY and risks drift when adding skills.
fix_direction: Use a single data-driven helper installFabricSkill(skillKey, templateRel, options) with skills represented as config objects in a metadata array.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Duplicated Process Liveness and Lock Reading Logic across Packages
description: isAlive(pid) and readLockState(projectRoot) are duplicated line-for-line between legacy-serve-lock-probe.ts (server) and serve-lock.ts (server-http-experimental). Redundant logic raises maintenance overhead and risks divergence if lock behavior changes.
fix_direction: Consolidate these OS/process utilities in the shared package (e.g. @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node lock-utils.ts) and import in both packages.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Standard Error throws bypass the actionHint error renderer
description: During fabric install, drift-abort and workspace folder checks throw plain Error instances. Lacking the structured actionHint field, renderTopLevelError treats them as generic 'other' errors, omitting recovery-guidance arrows/indents and leaving the developer with generic, un-actionable error stacks.
fix_direction: Throw a FabricError class (or structural FabricErrorShape match) carrying actionHint instead of generic Error.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Swallowed cache write failures silently break hooks and nudge logic
description: writeJsonState/writeTextState in state-store.cjs swallow all FS errors (read-only FS, permissions) and return boolean false with no logging/alert. Since the hook runs inside the editor process and never prints warnings, nudges constantly repeat or session-hints stop updating, with no way in fabric doctor to verify .fabric/.cache writability.
fix_direction: Have fabric doctor explicitly check write permissions/health of .fabric/.cache/ and log a diagnostic warning/error if the hook cache cannot be updated.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Severe light mode color contrast violations (line 192)
description: In light mode, warning tags/action headers use #f59e0b (Amber 500) on near-white (~2.1:1); small muted labels #71717a yield ~3.8:1. Fails WCAG 1.4.3 (4.5:1).
fix_direction: Use high-contrast light-mode overrides: Amber 700 (#b45309)/800 (#92400e) for warnings, darken muted text to zinc-600.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: CLI glyphs lack accessible text fallback in color-enabled sessions
description: In color-enabled terminals, status indicators are bare Unicode shapes (check/!/x) styled with ANSI color. Screen readers read these ambiguously (or skip them) and colorblind users lose the color channel, leaving only shape. Only NO_COLOR mode includes text fallback ([ok]/[warn]/[error]).
fix_direction: Prepend/append textual labels in all color states (e.g. '[ok] ✓', '[warn] !', '[error] x') for a robust visual + spoken fallback.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Store diagnostics severity check masks errors as info
description: In the store diagnostics renderer, any severity other than 'warn' (including critical errors) defaults to [info], making serious diagnostic errors appear as harmless info logs to screen-reader and terminal users, hiding critical issues.
fix_direction: Add explicit branches: severity==='error' -> symbol.error, 'warn' -> symbol.warn, else [info].

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: No PII scanning/redaction before capturing into KB or event ledger
description: secret-scan.ts only targets technical secrets (AWS/OpenAI/Slack/GitHub tokens, basic password assignments). There are no rules/heuristics to detect/redact PII (emails, IPs, names, phone numbers). Since event ledger inputs capture arbitrary caller free-text (intent, message, reason), any PII is permanently logged to plain-text events.jsonl or the KB without redaction.
fix_direction: Add dedicated PII regex rules (emails, IPs, phone numbers) into redactSecrets/scanForSecrets and apply across all telemetry logging and write-paths.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Audit gap: in-place knowledge edits (modifyEntry) emit no ledger event
description: When an in-place modification (title, summary, maturity, tags, relevance) is performed on a pending/canonical entry, reviewKnowledge dispatches to modifyEntry. Other actions (approve/reject/layer-flip/defer) emit telemetry to events.jsonl, but in-place edits write directly to frontmatter and exit WITHOUT a ledger event — allowing users or skills to silently change maturity levels or relevance paths.
fix_direction: Emit a knowledge_modified / knowledge_metadata_updated event in the modifyEntry in-place flow capturing stable_id/path, patch changes, original values, and session metadata.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: metrics.jsonl grows unbounded with NO rotation mechanism
description: Metric-natured events flush to .fabric/metrics.jsonl every 60s. Unlike events.jsonl (which has rotation + age cutoff), metrics.jsonl has NO rotation/age cutoff/truncation and appends indefinitely. readMetrics loads and parses the entire file into memory, causing linear memory/CPU degradation as it grows.
fix_direction: Add size-based and age-based rotation for metrics.jsonl like events.jsonl (archive old rows to a compressed sidecar, keep only active rows).

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Full-ledger in-memory scans for events and metrics files scale poorly
description: readEventLedger and readMetrics read their entire .jsonl into memory and split into lines, running JSON.parse + Zod validation per line. This happens during doctor health checks (inspectEventsJsonlGates on every CLI invocation/server loop), scaling O(M) with ledger size (up to 50MB before rotation), causing memory spikes and CPU choking.
fix_direction: Implement index/tail-based streaming scans (read only last N lines or since a timestamp) or a pre-aggregated in-memory index, avoiding full-text parse per call.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Concurrent multi-window sessions cause cross-session selection-token invalidation
description: selectionTokenCache is a single global in-memory Map; tokens are bound to the project's revision_hash and cleared immediately on any file mutation. In multi-window concurrent sessions, one window modifying a knowledge file (sync or frontmatter edit) changes the revision hash, instantly invalidating active selection tokens in ALL other windows, aborting reasoning loops with 'selection_token is missing or expired'.
fix_direction: Keep active selection tokens valid for their full TTL (30 min) despite minor revision_hash mutations, or scope invalidation to the specific files mutated rather than globally destroying all active tokens.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Synchronous blocking FS and process execution in multi-store core
description: initStore, listMarkdown, listStoreKnowledge, readKnowledgeAcrossStores, aggregatePendingAcrossStores use synchronous fs APIs (readdirSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, unlinkSync) and synchronous execFileSync('git'). These block Node's single-threaded event loop; as store count or corpus grows or under concurrent sessions, the loop chokes, blocking all other HTTP/MCP requests.
fix_direction: Refactor multi-store core to async (fs.promises, execFile-as-promise) to prevent event-loop blockage during cross-store reads/writes or git interactions.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Prompt Injection Sanitization Bypass via recent_paths and evidence_paths
description: The prompt-injection sanitization gate in fab_extract_knowledge strips injection patterns from summary, session_context, must_read_if, intent_clues, slug — but omits recent_paths and evidence_paths. If those arrays carry injection text (newlines with instructions) they are persisted directly into the ## Evidence markdown block or evidence_paths frontmatter unsanitized.
fix_direction: Extend sanitizeInjectionFields to also sanitize elements of recent_paths and evidence_paths arrays.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: N+1 Sequential Disk Reads during Knowledge Sync Drift Checks
description: During knowledge sync drift checks (ensureKnowledgeFresh, reconcileKnowledge), the sync orchestrator traverses every knowledge file sequentially via processSingleFile(), doing await stat then await readFile per entry. In larger KBs this leads to slow Planning and Recall MCP tool response times.
fix_direction: Batch file parsing concurrently with Promise.all() + a concurrency controller (p-limit) instead of sequential I/O.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Graceful read-path auto-heal crashes on malformed metadata
description: In loadActiveMetaOrStale (graceful read path for planContext hints), a malformed agents.meta.json makes readAgentsMeta throw AgentsMetaInvalidError. loadActiveMetaOrStale catches rebuild errors but explicitly lets AgentsMetaInvalidError propagate, crashing all plan-context hint requests and breaking the graceful-fallback guarantee.
fix_direction: Catch AgentsMetaInvalidError inside loadActiveMetaOrStale, quarantine the corrupt file, and trigger a fresh meta rebuild instead of throwing.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Mismatched multi-file sync of agents.meta.json and knowledge-test.index.json
description: writeKnowledgeMeta writes agents.meta.json and .cache/knowledge-test.index.json sequentially via atomicWriteText with no lock spanning both. Two concurrent executions can interleave (A writes meta X, B writes meta Y, B writes index Y, A writes index X), producing on-disk state where metadata does not match the cached test index.
fix_direction: Enforce a serialized lock covering the entire multi-file write sequence in writeKnowledgeMeta for transactional multi-file updates.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Silent index drift on contended git index.lock
description: During auto-archiving/approvals/rejections, doctor.ts and review.ts run git mv/git rm. A stale .git/index.lock (crashed git or concurrent command) makes git ops throw; the try/catch falls back to plain fs.rename/unlink but silently degrades git status — files are moved/removed on disk yet tracked incorrectly in the git index.
fix_direction: Proactively detect a stale .git/index.lock, warn/clean up, and report git index sync issues prominently in fabric doctor instead of silently degrading.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Circular Dependency Cycle in Doctor Services
description: Circular dependency between doctor.ts and doctor-cite-coverage.ts: doctor.ts imports from doctor-cite-coverage.js (lines 8203, 8223) while doctor-cite-coverage.ts imports inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift and normalizePath back from doctor.js (line 68). Resolved at call-time but tightly couples the domains and complicates testing/tree-shaking.
fix_direction: Move shared utilities (inspectL1BootstrapSnapshotDrift, normalizePath) and core types into a common doctor-utils.ts both modules import, eliminating the cycle.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Cloned Dead Code and Unused Imports in doctor-cite-coverage.ts
description: doctor-cite-coverage.ts has a large dead-code import block copied verbatim when split out of doctor.ts, leaving unused imports (execFileSync, spawnSync from node:child_process; mkdirSync, readdirSync from node:fs; access, appendFile, mkdir, readFile, rename, unlink, writeFile from node:fs/promises; homedir from node:os; Script from node:vm).
fix_direction: Prune the unused imports and Node module dependencies from the file header.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Brittle Class Identity Checks in HTTP Server Error Handling
description: The HTTP server error handler relies on error instanceof FabricError. Crossing package/bundle boundaries in a monorepo can make instanceof fail due to multiple class symbol instances. The CLI resolved this with structural duck-typing (hasActionHint), but the HTTP server still uses fragile class identity matching.
fix_direction: Migrate HTTP server error normalization to the same structural type-guard (hasActionHint) exported by the shared package.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Inconsistent and Bloated Error/Return Contracts in Doctor APIs
description: The doctor service surfaces three report types (DoctorReport, DoctorFixReport, DoctorApplyLintReport) with slightly different fields representing the same concept using disparate keys (fixable_errors vs fixed vs mutations; manual_errors vs remaining_manual_errors), and all duplicate the full raw report: DoctorReport inside themselves — API bloat and high integration complexity.
fix_direction: Consolidate around a single layered schema (unified DoctorReport with an optional remedy/mutation transaction block) rather than three divergent envelopes.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Blocking package install with no feedback during local MCP server install
description: In local MCP install mode, installLocalFabricServer spawns a synchronous pnpm add/npm install that blocks the terminal for 10-30s with no spinner, dots, or package-manager output. The developer sees a complete freeze and may abort thinking it hung.
fix_direction: Add a progressive spinner (@clack/prompts) or pipe the subprocess stdio to stdout/stderr in real-time during the long install.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Inconsistent --auto requirement and mutex restrictions for doctor mutations
description: --enrich-descriptions requires --auto to mutate, whereas --fix and --fix-knowledge mutate immediately without --auto; additionally --fix and --fix-knowledge are mutually exclusive, preventing a single comprehensive repair run. Confusing flag semantics.
fix_direction: Make --enrich-descriptions mutate directly with symmetric dry-run/interactive prompts, or add a unified --fix-all that chains all fixes sequentially.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Ambiguous MCP tool warnings lack a semantic message for AI consumers
description: MCP tools (fab_recall, fab_plan_context, fab_archive_scan, fab_review) return structured warnings with code and action_hint but no descriptive message explaining WHY. On mcp_payload_warn / mcp_payload_trimmed the AI cannot tell the exact byte overflow or dropped count to adjust its request.
fix_direction: Extend StructuredToolWarning + appendPayloadWarning to include a descriptive message detailing the exact limit breached or action taken.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Dual-TTY check disables color when stderr is redirected
description: isColorEnabled disables ANSI color globally if either stdout OR stderr is not a TTY. When stderr is redirected (fabric doctor 2> err.log) color is disabled on stdout even though the console is color-capable, degrading readability.
fix_direction: Evaluate each stream's target independently (check process.stdout.isTTY for stdout-bound prints) rather than the boolean intersection of both streams.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unicode arrows and em-dashes corrupt on legacy terminals and clutter screen readers
description: The CLI doctor command renders literal Unicode arrows (->) and em-dashes directly to stdout. In legacy/non-UTF-8 terminals (Windows CMD with GBK code page) these garble; screen readers read them literally, adding clutter — contradicting the project's own ASCII guideline in error-render.ts.
fix_direction: Use ASCII equivalents (-> for arrows, colon/double-hyphen for em-dash) following error-render.ts.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: No retention/rotation/auto-deletion for archived event ledger and KB files
description: rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded rotates/compresses the active events.jsonl into .fabric/events.archive/ (events-rotated-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl[.gz]), but there is no cleanup/expiration for those archives — they grow indefinitely. Likewise, pruned knowledge entries moved to .fabric/.archive/ by doctor are retained forever with no purge.
fix_direction: Add a retention cleanup task (in fabric doctor or the rotation daemon) that removes archives in .fabric/events.archive/ and .fabric/.archive/ exceeding a configured max age (e.g. 90/180 days).

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Provenance / git history loss on layer flip or cross-store transfer
description: When a KT-* entry flips to KP-* in modifyLayerFlip, the original file is git rm'd and rewritten under the personal root (~/.fabric/knowledge), a separate/untracked git repo — so the original commit/development history is lost. The same history loss occurs moving entries across multi-store boundaries.
fix_direction: Inject a provenance_history frontmatter field (original store UUID, stable ID, prior commit hashes) on cross-layer/store transfer, or record a knowledge_transferred event documenting the provenance chain.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: O(N x R x T) nested-loop cartesian product for path-locality scoring
description: scoreDescriptionItem nests a loop over a candidate's relevance_paths (R) and the session's targetPaths (T). Over N candidates this is O(N x R x T). When a session targets many files (full-repo analysis/batch) or candidates list many relevance paths, this nested iteration degrades quadratically at scale.
fix_direction: Pre-index targetPaths by directory/package for O(1) locality lookup instead of an O(R x T) nested scan per candidate.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Excessive per-item file appends during batch knowledge selections
description: getKnowledgeSections for a batch of K stable IDs iterates each ID and executes a separate appendEventLedgerEvent (knowledge_consumed) — K separate disk appends, each with Zod validation, string conversion, dir existence checks, size stat, disk write. For many selected rules this is an I/O bottleneck and swells events.jsonl quickly.
fix_direction: Batch all knowledge_consumed events into a single atomic array write to the ledger rather than per-rule append promises in a loop.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Uncapped audit cursor cache leaks memory across multi-project roots
description: ContextCache holds raw metadata and cursors in global maps. metaSlot/contextSlot are TTL-based, but auditSlot (AuditCursor per project root) is never cleared and has no TTL/capacity limit. In long-running MCP server processes accessing multiple project roots or temporary worktrees, this leaks memory growing with the number of roots accessed.
fix_direction: Add a max-capacity LRU eviction (like the selection token cache) or a TTL for inactive project roots on auditSlot.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unclear and Cryptic Core Type Names in Agents Metadata
description: Core counter schemas use cryptic acronyms. In AgentsMetaCountersEnvelope, counters sit under KP and KT (personal/team layers) using keys MOD, DEC, GLD, PIT, PRO (models, decisions, guidelines, pitfalls, processes), making the code harder to read/maintain for new developers.
fix_direction: Rename interface keys to descriptive names (personal/team instead of KP/KT; models/decisions/... instead of MOD/DEC) or provide clear documentation/mappings in the type files.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Sidebar navigation lacks a landmark identifier
description: The sidebar <nav> has no aria-label/landmark description, so screen-reader landmark navigation cannot distinguish it from other nav bars — WCAG 1.3.1.
fix_direction: Add aria-label="Sidebar Navigation" to the sidebar nav element.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-102040 (engine=agy, round 1; dedup deferred per user)

title: Silent truncation of plan-context candidates via hardcoded Top-K cap (default 24)
description: planContext candidates are silently truncated to a top-K cap (PLAN_CONTEXT_TOP_K_DEFAULT=24). Although omitted_candidate_count warns more exist, relevant knowledge beyond the top 24 is dropped. In a 10x-100x corpus where many rules share close BM25/locality scores, this hard cap causes silent omission and degraded reasoning context.
fix_direction: Dynamic budgeting by token weight/payload size, or let the AI paginate/request more when omitted_candidate_count > 0, instead of a hardcoded top-K.
